title: Health Monitor — Status Tracking
description: Create a HealthMonitor class that tracks server metrics. Track: uptime (seconds since start), connections_total, connections_active, requests_per_minute (using a sliding window), and errors_total. Use collections.deque with a maxlen to keep a sliding window of recent request timestamps for calculating requests_per_minute. Include record_connection(), record_disconnect(), record_request(), record_error(), get_status() returning a dict of all metrics, and is_healthy() returning True if errors_total < 100 and connections_active < 1000.
test_code:
# Tests for Step 9: HealthMonitor
import time

assert 'HealthMonitor' in dir(), 'HealthMonitor class must be defined'

monitor = HealthMonitor()

# Test required methods exist
assert callable(getattr(monitor, 'record_connection', None)), 'Must have record_connection'
assert callable(getattr(monitor, 'record_disconnect', None)), 'Must have record_disconnect'
assert callable(getattr(monitor, 'record_request', None)), 'Must have record_request'
assert callable(getattr(monitor, 'record_error', None)), 'Must have record_error'
assert callable(getattr(monitor, 'get_status', None)), 'Must have get_status'
assert callable(getattr(monitor, 'is_healthy', None)), 'Must have is_healthy'

# Test initial state
status = monitor.get_status()
assert isinstance(status, dict), 'get_status should return a dict'
assert status['connections_total'] == 0, 'connections_total should start at 0'
assert status['connections_active'] == 0, 'connections_active should start at 0'
assert status['errors_total'] == 0, 'errors_total should start at 0'
assert 'uptime' in status, 'status should include uptime'
assert 'requests_per_minute' in status, 'status should include requests_per_minute'

# Test recording connections
monitor.record_connection()
monitor.record_connection()
monitor.record_connection()
status = monitor.get_status()
assert status['connections_total'] == 3, 'connections_total should be 3'
assert status['connections_active'] == 3, 'connections_active should be 3'

# Test disconnect
monitor.record_disconnect()
status = monitor.get_status()
assert status['connections_active'] == 2, 'connections_active should be 2 after disconnect'
assert status['connections_total'] == 3, 'connections_total should still be 3'

# Test recording requests
for _ in range(10):
    monitor.record_request()
status = monitor.get_status()
assert status['requests_per_minute'] >= 10, 'requests_per_minute should count recent requests'

# Test recording errors
monitor.record_error()
monitor.record_error()
status = monitor.get_status()
assert status['errors_total'] == 2, 'errors_total should be 2'

# Test is_healthy
assert monitor.is_healthy() is True, 'Server should be healthy with low errors and connections'

# Test uptime is non-negative
assert status['uptime'] >= 0, 'uptime should be non-negative'
acceptance_criteria: HealthMonitor initializes all counters to zero and records start time
record_connection() increments both connections_total and connections_active
record_request() adds a timestamp to the sliding window deque
get_status() returns a dict with uptime, connections_total, connections_active, requests_per_minute, errors_total
is_healthy() returns True when errors < 100 and active connections < 1000
hints: Use time.time() for both start time and request timestamps.
For requests_per_minute: count entries in the deque where time.time() - timestamp < 60.
Use deque(maxlen=1000) to automatically discard old entries beyond the window.
